claims.claim: README serves as the project entry point with role-based documentation links
claims.actual_source: New docs/INDEX.md preserves role-based navigation table

claims.claim: README documents CI badge and quick-start command
claims.actual_source: Badge and quick-start retained in modified README.md root

claims.claim: README links to sub-docs by audience (Operator, Developer, SRE)
claims.actual_source: docs/INDEX.md carries audience-role table; cross-links updated

claims.claim: README references docs/ directory as the canonical documentation root
claims.actual_source: Still correct — new docs/ tree at same path

claims.claim: dist/ directory ships a pre-built offline bundle (.zip) ready to transfer
claims.actual_source: dist/ is empty in current repo; scripts/build_offline_bundle.sh must be run first to produce the zip

claims.claim: macOS installation path and launchd service documented under §1.3
claims.actual_source: macOS target was never implemented; only Linux (systemd) and Windows (NSSM) service paths exist

claims.claim: preflight.ps1 validates host environment before install on Windows
claims.actual_source: deploy/preflight.ps1 present and referenced in install.ps1

claims.claim: install.ps1 preserves config.json, alerts.json, rule_schedules.json on upgrade
claims.actual_source: Confirmed in install.ps1 logic and UPGRADE.md; operator-owned files are never overwritten

claims.claim: Python 3.10+ required; pip install -r requirements.txt
claims.actual_source: requirements.txt confirmed; Python 3.10+ union-type syntax used throughout src/

claims.claim: NSSM bundled at deploy\nssm.exe for Windows service registration
claims.actual_source: deploy/nssm.exe present; install.ps1 references it

claims.claim: Run GET /health as a preflight check before upgrading to verify PCE connectivity
claims.actual_source: No /health endpoint exists; actual health check is GET /api/status (or /api/v2/health on the PCE directly)

claims.claim: setup-prod-git.sh does not exist in the repo
claims.actual_source: Commit 2f173d0 added scripts/setup-prod-git.sh; it does exist

claims.claim: Migration scripts in scripts/ are idempotent and safe to re-run
claims.actual_source: All migration scripts confirmed idempotent by design (upsert / check-before-alter patterns)

claims.claim: Operator-owned files (config.json, alerts.json, rule_schedules.json) are never overwritten during upgrade
claims.actual_source: install.ps1 and upgrade path confirmed; new fields must be merged manually from config.json.example

claims.claim: Upgrade requires stopping the service before extracting new bundle
claims.actual_source: Documented procedure matches install.ps1 and systemd stop steps

claims.claim: Dashboard includes a 'Multi-PCE switcher' widget for quick profile switching
claims.actual_source: No such widget in current index.html; profile switching is in Settings → PCE Profiles, not the dashboard

claims.claim: CLI `pce` command triggers profile switch and reinitializes ApiClient
claims.actual_source: No standalone `pce` CLI subcommand; profile management is via interactive shell settings menu or GUI /api/pce-profiles endpoints

claims.claim: Settings page covers API credentials, alert channels, timezone, language/theme, and PCE profile management
claims.actual_source: Settings manager confirmed in src/gui; all sections present

claims.claim: Rule Scheduler shows [SKIP] when rule or parent Ruleset is in Draft state
claims.actual_source: Confirmed in scheduler log output and Troubleshooting section

claims.claim: Policy Usage report shows 0 hits if rules are draft-only
claims.actual_source: Only active (provisioned) rules are queried by the PCE API; user must provision first

claims.claim: config.json holds active profile credentials plus pce_profiles array for multi-PCE
claims.actual_source: config.json schema confirmed: api block + active_pce_id + pce_profiles array

claims.claim: PDF output uses pure-Python ReportLab library bundled in the offline zip
claims.actual_source: Commit 92143a6 removed ReportLab; commit e90c373 made fmt=pdf an alias for print-ready HTML; 9acedda removed pdf_exporter.py and the ReportLab dependency

claims.claim: Report formats available: HTML, XLSX, CSV, PDF
claims.actual_source: PDF is now an alias for HTML (browser-native print); ReportLab PDF generation removed; 'pdf' format produces print-optimised HTML

claims.claim: Four report types: Traffic, Audit, VEN Status, Policy Usage
claims.actual_source: CLI subcommands report generate-traffic/audit/ven-status/policy-usage confirmed

claims.claim: Report generation is triggered via CLI or scheduled via rule_schedules.json
claims.actual_source: Both paths confirmed in src/cli/report.py and scheduler

claims.claim: NSSM ships in offline bundle and PDF uses ReportLab (repeated in Installation context)
claims.actual_source: NSSM shipping verified; ReportLab claim obsolete (removed)

claims.claim: Security rules are organized in B-series (Baseline) and other lettered series with named thresholds
claims.actual_source: Rule definitions confirmed in src/; B-series rules use threshold keys in config (e.g., cross_env_connection_threshold, user_destination_threshold)

claims.claim: Rule B009 triggers when cross-environment flow count exceeds cross_env_connection_threshold (default 100)
claims.actual_source: Threshold key and default value confirmed in source and legacy doc

claims.claim: Rule B007 triggers when a single user contacts more than user_destination_threshold distinct IPs (default 20)
claims.actual_source: Confirmed in rule implementation and legacy doc

claims.claim: Exact total count of security rules as stated in legacy doc
claims.actual_source: Legacy doc rule count not spot-checked against current src/; new tree received the content but count needs verification

claims.claim: Rule threshold keys are configurable in config.json under security_rules block
claims.actual_source: config.json.example confirms security_rules threshold block

claims.claim: JSON format handled by JSONLineFormatter class
claims.actual_source: Commit edda47b added NormalizedJSONFormatter as the actual JSON formatter; JSONLineFormatter still exists but NormalizedJSONFormatter is used for syslog/HEC JSON output

claims.claim: SIEM destination configured via single endpoint: 'host:port' field
claims.actual_source: Commits 4ceb5bd / 1a1f99d replaced single endpoint string with separate host and port fields; legacy configs are auto-migrated on first load

claims.claim: Supported transports: UDP syslog, TCP syslog, TLS syslog, Splunk HEC
claims.actual_source: All four transports confirmed in src/siem/transports/

claims.claim: SIEM forwarder requires PCE cache to be enabled; data flows through siem_dispatch table
claims.actual_source: Confirmed in siem dispatcher and pce_cache dependency check

claims.claim: DLQ (dead-letter queue) subcommands available: replay, purge, list
claims.actual_source: CLI siem dlq/replay/purge subcommands confirmed in cli-command-map and src/cli/siem.py

claims.claim: CEF format uses official Illumio field names for flow events
claims.actual_source: Commits 466f771 and 797926c aligned CEF fields with official Illumio traffic summary log format

claims.claim: Background sections on PCE/VEN platform are accurate Illumio product descriptions
claims.actual_source: Platform background (PCE four tiers, VEN enforcement modes, workload types) is product-level documentation that remains factually correct

claims.claim: ~55KB of legacy platform background detail is load-bearing for migration
claims.actual_source: New architecture/overview.md is a complete rewrite; legacy content is reference background only, not structurally imported

claims.claim: Four VEN enforcement modes: Idle, Visibility Only, Selective, Full
claims.actual_source: Enforcement mode table confirmed in legacy doc and matches current Illumio 25.4 documentation

claims.claim: Directory structure and module deep-dive sections reflect current src/ layout
claims.actual_source: src/ has grown significantly; legacy §2 directory structure is partially outdated (missing siem/, i18n/, new CLI modules)

claims.claim: Multi-PCE profile architecture documented with config.json schema
claims.actual_source: Config schema (api block + active_pce_id + pce_profiles) confirmed current

claims.claim: Cache stored in cache/ directory relative to project root
claims.actual_source: Commit a88e823 moved cache to data/pce_cache.sqlite; pce_cache.db_path default is 'data/pce_cache.sqlite'

claims.claim: PCE cache enabled via pce_cache.enabled = true in config.json
claims.actual_source: Config key confirmed in config.json.example and pce_cache module

claims.claim: Cache supports configurable retention: events_retention_days, traffic_raw_retention_days, traffic_agg_retention_days
claims.actual_source: All three retention keys confirmed in config schema and cache manager

claims.claim: cache retention --run CLI command available for manual retention sweep
claims.actual_source: CLI subcommand confirmed in cli-command-map (cache retention)

claims.claim: SIEM dispatcher depends on PCE cache being enabled (reads from siem_dispatch table populated by cache)
claims.actual_source: Dependency confirmed in SIEM integration and pce_cache docs

claims.claim: Rate limiting capped at rate_limit_per_minute (max 500 PCE API calls/min)
claims.actual_source: Config key confirmed; max 500 noted in config.json.example comments

claims.claim: PCE API has no versioning prefix on org-scoped endpoints (e.g., /orgs/{id}/events)
claims.actual_source: Confirmed: org-scoped endpoints use /orgs/{id}/... without /api/v2; only resource-level GETs use /api/v2{href}

claims.claim: Traffic queries use async pattern: POST to async_queries, poll status, then GET download
claims.actual_source: Three-step async query flow confirmed in src/api/ and API endpoint table

claims.claim: PCE health check endpoint is /api/v2/health returning 200
claims.actual_source: Endpoint confirmed in API endpoint reference table

claims.claim: Workload label update uses PUT to /api/v2{workload_href} with {labels: [{href}]} body
claims.actual_source: Request body shape confirmed in API cookbook and src/api/client.py

claims.claim: List Active Rulesets endpoint: /orgs/{id}/sec_policy/active/rule_sets?max_results=10000
claims.actual_source: Endpoint and parameter confirmed in API reference table

claims.claim: Standard Illumio platform terms defined: PCE, VEN, Workload (Managed/Unmanaged/Container), Label, Ruleset
claims.actual_source: All core terms present in new docs/reference/glossary.md; sourced from legacy Glossary.md

claims.claim: Glossary cross-links to Architecture.md for PCE/VEN deep-dives
claims.actual_source: Legacy cross-links point to Architecture.md; new glossary should link to docs/architecture/overview.md

claims.claim: i18n glossary.json in src/i18n/data/ is the single SoT for terms that must stay English in zh_TW
claims.actual_source: src/i18n/data/glossary.json confirmed with 16 entries (PCE, VEN, Workload, etc.)

claims.claim: All four label dimensions documented: role, app, env, loc
claims.actual_source: Four label dimensions confirmed in Glossary and Architecture background

claims.claim: 'Policy Usage 0 hits' root cause is cache not populated
claims.actual_source: Actual root cause: rules are draft-only (not provisioned); PCE API only returns hits for active/provisioned rules; cache population is a separate issue

claims.claim: Windows NSSM troubleshooting steps documented under Troubleshooting section
claims.actual_source: NSSM troubleshooting is better organized under Installation; new docs/getting-started.md covers it; should not be duplicated in troubleshooting

claims.claim: externally-managed-environment pip error on Ubuntu 22.04+ / Debian 12+ requires venv workaround
claims.actual_source: PEP 668 issue confirmed; venv workaround documented in Troubleshooting and getting-started

claims.claim: PCE profile switch has no effect if ApiClient is not reinitialized
claims.actual_source: Root cause confirmed: must use GUI Activate button or CLI profile switch (not manual config edit) to trigger reinitialization

claims.claim: Report shows all VENs as online when hours_since_last_heartbeat not returned by older PCE version
claims.actual_source: Confirmed as a known PCE API version compatibility issue

claims.claim: Top-level subcommands and deprecated aliases documented (e.g., report traffic → report generate-traffic)
claims.actual_source: All subcommand aliases confirmed in cli-command-map and src/cli/; new docs/reference/cli.md received this content

claims.claim: Legacy argparse flags --monitor and --gui are deprecated aliases for monitor and gui subcommands
claims.actual_source: Aliases confirmed in illumio-ops.py dispatcher (_CLICK_SUBCOMMANDS set and argparse fallback)

claims.claim: Adding a new subcommand requires registering in _CLICK_SUBCOMMANDS set in illumio-ops.py
claims.actual_source: Confirmed in illumio-ops.py; missing registration causes fallthrough to legacy argparse handler

claims.claim: Interactive shell menu paths documented alongside CLI flags for each subcommand
claims.actual_source: cli-command-map.md table includes both CLI and shell → menu-number paths; new cli.md preserved this

claims.claim: Three fonts vendored: Space Grotesk (variable woff2), Inter (latin subset variable woff2), JetBrains Mono (latin subset variable woff2)
claims.actual_source: All three woff2 files confirmed in src/gui/static/fonts/; sourcing notes match fontsource package versions

claims.claim: Inter uses latin-subset from @fontsource-variable/inter v5.2.8 to meet <280 KB total font budget
claims.actual_source: Fontsource package version and size target confirmed in fonts-vendoring.md sourcing notes

claims.claim: JetBrains Mono official release zip has no variable woff2 (only static woff2 + variable ttf), so fontsource used instead
claims.actual_source: Sourcing rationale confirmed in legacy doc; JetBrains/JetBrainsMono v2.304 release structure matches

claims.claim: SpaceGrotesk-VF.woff2 sourced from floriankarsten/space-grotesk GitHub repo, weight range 300-700
claims.actual_source: Source repo and weight range confirmed in legacy doc sourcing notes
